calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Defendant/Appellants Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award And To Enter Judgment Against Defendant/Appellant
Off Calendar Nov-08-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Katherine Manning, Esq. of Dept. of Industrial Relations, Tel. 510.926.0860, appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff Rodney Jackson;
Aaron Hancock, Esq. of Mokri Vanis Jones LLP, Tel. 925.375.1859, appearing in person for Defendant Bauer's Intelligent Transportation, Inc.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF RODNEY JACKSON's Motion To Dismiss Defendant/Appellants Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award And To Enter Judgment Against Defendant/Appellant.

This matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to dismiss, enter judgment, and obtain fees is granted.

On July 12, 2024, the court ordered: "Defendant's unopposed motion to set aside and vacate the November 20, 2023 judgment is granted. Defendant Bauer has leave to file a notice of appeal and answer within 10 court days of the filing of this order." Defendant filed this written order on September 24, 2024, and timely filed its de novo appeal the next day. Defendant, however, did not post the undertaking before filing its appeal. Defendant therefore failed to comply with Labor Code section 98.2(b) and there was nothing in this court's September 24, 2024, order that relieved defendant of this requirement. Indeed, the requirement is mandatory and jurisdictional. "As a condition to filing an appeal pursuant to this section, an employer shall first post an undertaking with the reviewing court in the amount of the order, decision, or award." (Labor Code sec. 98.2(b); Palagin v. Paniagua Construction, Inc. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 124, 126 ["We conclude that the statutory deadline for an employer to post an undertaking, which the Legislature has identified as 'a condition to' the filing of the notice that commences the court's jurisdiction, is a jurisdictional deadline that cannot be extended by the trial court. (sec. 98.2(b).) We therefore agree with Palagin that his motion to dismiss the proceeding should have been granted, and we reverse the judgment."].)

Defendant's other arguments founder. Plaintiff is not seeking reconsideration of the court's September 24, 2024, order. While there are overlapping issues, plaintiff seeks new affirmative relief based on subsequent events. Palagin recognizes the propriety of a motion to dismiss in these circumstances (see above) and such relief is warranted per the court's inherent powers. (CCP 128(a)(8).) Defendant failed to perfect the appeal in compliance with the Labor Code and the court must dispose of the case. Plaintiff shall prepare a judgment in his favor.

Pursuant to Labor Code sec. 98.2(c), plaintiff is entitled to reasonable costs since defendant's appeal was unsuccessful. Plaintiff's request for $2,000 for the costs of this motion are granted.

Order signed. Separate judgment signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Alice Wilbur, CSR 538, Tel. 714.267.9863, Email: qnfhvnwilbur@aol.com. Bailiff: Park. (301/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Defendant/Appellants Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award And To Enter Judgment Against Defendant/Appellant
Off Calendar Oct-14-2024 Continued To Nov-06-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BAUERS INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment.

Defendant's unopposed motion to set aside and vacate the November 20, 2023 judgment is granted. Defendant Bauer has leave to file a notice of appeal and answer within 10 court days of the filing of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order/statement of decision which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk: W. Trupek/R. Woods; Not Reported (302/RCE)
